title: Living in Boulder vlog 3 - 21 - 2017: Bike Racer Levels Pages RAAM

enrichment.summary: Kyle records an early-morning vlog from Boulder while working intensely on 'bike racer levels pages' for his website, including a Race Across America (RAAM) page. He discusses his financial stress (~$800 credit card debt, low checking balance), his plans to grow a Patreon, his vision for a futuristic information-dense room interface inspired by Star Trek and Black Mirror, and his broader project of building a meticulously detailed personal website he compares to a spaceship that must be perfect before he can 'launch' into other goals.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Must be flawless like a spaceship or distractions will derail him entirely
Envisions entire rooms covered in color-coded, flashing text displays for maximum information absorption
Believes humanity will evolve toward jam-packed, color-coded text interfaces for faster data intake
Should be taught as a class in school

stances.topic: current work on bike racer levels pages
stances.position: Mentally exhausting and unsustainable long-term, like holding his breath underwater, but necessary

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently living in Boulder and it's 5:47 AM on March 21st.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was planning to do a Black History video a couple of days ago but ended up focusing on food instead.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel like I'm just spinning out from working constantly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The work I'm doing is tedious and hard to explain, making it seem like I'm not doing anything.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm making big lists and will be able to explain my work better once I reach the next level.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Working on my bike race project is particularly difficult but also fun for me.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not currently riding my bike, which is making me feel a bit off.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Working on my bike race project feels like working underwater holding my breath—I can only do it for a limited time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm questioning how I'm managing my life right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to finish and compile my work tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been collecting time trials around water in Boulder because they fascinate me.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Boulder feels like a spaceship because you can race people from all over the world here.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm integrating these time trials into my overall bike race levels, which include flats, ups, and downs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to historical audio to prepare for a history test.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I find predicting the future really interesting and think it should be taught as a class.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been watching 'Black Mirror,' which explores possible future scenarios.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I imagine a future where walls are covered in screens displaying information like in 'Star Trek.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In the future, we'll have websites packed with text, colors, and borders to maximize data intake for speed reading.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I envision entire rooms covered in screens flashing information in color-coded text.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think printed paper could be connected to the internet, flashing updates like names when people are online.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Working on my bike race project is mentally exhausting, but physically I feel great.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I know I'll need to take a break from the bike race project soon but will return to finish it later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After my break, I plan to work on adding photos to my audio history books to help with memorization.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to memorize my entire 3,300-page history book.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Adding pictures to my website will help me learn and remember the content better.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to fix the links on my website to make it more polished.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm in $800 of credit card debt with 19% interest and only $200 in my checking account.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to monetize my website through Patreon to generate income.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I made this video because I couldn't sleep and was too tired to work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on a 'Race Across America' page for my website, which is taking a long time because I'm not cutting corners.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need my website to be perfect so I can stay focused and not get distracted.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I compare my need for a perfect website to astronauts needing everything to be flawless for their trajectory.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If there's any flaw in my website, I might lose focus and start something else.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need my website to be like a sealed spaceship so I can 'launch' into other projects.
